facts.text: Boston was founded in 1630 on the Shawmut Peninsula by English Puritan settlers who named it after Boston, Lincolnshire, England.
The Boston Public Library, America's first free library, opened in 1852 and remains a cornerstone of the city’s public knowledge.
Boston Latin School, founded in 1635, is the oldest public school in the United States and still educates students today.
Back Bay was created by filling the area with gravel from Needham and earth from leveling Boston’s original hills, a massive 19th‑century engineering feat.
First Church in Boston, founded in 1630, is the city’s oldest church and a testament to the early Puritan community.
The Boston Celtics, founded in 1946, have won eighteen NBA championships, the most of any team in league history.
